channel.title: Riddim Yard
channel.description:
Riddim Yard is the home of foundation Jamaican music: Ska, Rocksteady, Deejay, Dub, Roots & Dancehall 
 
email: rickhowe@riddimyard.com.au

item.title: Jesus Dread - The annual Good Friday tribute to Yabby You
item.description:
The annual Good Friday tribute to Yabby You - the Jesus Dread. The heaviest producers in roots reggae history, responsible for some of the most spiritual and militant sounds ever pressed.

He earned the name Jesus Dread because, despite wearing dreadlocks, he rejected the Rastafari belief in Haile Selassie as the second coming, holding instead that Jesus Christ was the only true messiah. That stance set him apart - and gave him the name that stuck.

We also preview what’s coming for Record Store Day on April 18 via VP Records, including two heavyweight reissues: Congo Ashanti (1979) from The Congos, and a killer instrumental organ set from Jackie Mittoo, mixed at King Tubby's Studio in 1978.

Plus newly remastered tracks landing on streaming via VP from Dennis Brown, Eek-A-Mouse and Garnet Silk.

And new music from Stand High Patrol - ‘Walk Walk Walk’. The Dubadub Musketeerz stretch into new territory, blending roots reggae, UK dub and the house sounds of New York and Chicago. A tale of a nocturnal stroll, a moment suspended in time - the first single from their forthcoming LP Skanking & Jacking.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Thompson Sound pre-releases mixed by Roberto Sanchez
item.description:
A showcase of forthcoming Thompson Sound titles on pre-release vinyl - classic Linval Thompson productions with the Roots Radics, recorded at Channel One Studio in the early 80s.

Remixed from the original master tapes by Roberto Sánchez at his Lone Ark studio in Santander, Spain, bringing new depth to some heavyweight cuts.

Featuring special mixes of Poorman Style (1982) by Barrington Levy, Peaceful Man (1982) from Freddie McGregor, and Perilous Times (1979) by Mystic Eyes, alongside new vocal cuts from Rafeelya and Nadia McAnuff.

Plus the new single ‘Ting Loud’ from Protoje featuring Masicka, taken from the forthcoming album The Art Of Acceptance, out April 17 on Ineffable Records. A killer blend of 80s Augustus "Gussie" Clarke-style production with a modern twist.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Winston Reedy returns
item.description:
Killer new Lovers Rock mixed by Roberto Sanchez ft. Cimarons singer Winston Reedy on the new 45 on Pimienta Records in Spain titled You Got To Believe. 

Plus more forthcoming Thompson Sound titles on pre-release vinyl - classic Linval Thompson productions with the Roots Radics, recorded at Channel One Studio in the early 80s.

Remixed from the original master tapes by Roberto Sánchez at his Lone Ark studio in Santander, Spain, bringing new depth to some heavyweight cuts.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: More fire from the Lone Ark
item.description:
Buju Banton sounds recharged on the new single 'Butterflies' - a slick return to the dancehall style that made the Gargamel one of Jamaica’s most feared voices. Classic energy, modern touch, and a reminder that few can ride a riddim like Buju.

Fresh roots pressure comes from Spain’s Bass Lee Music with Benjammin - 'Fire Burn', backed by the BLM Players and mixed by Roberto Sánchez in analogue style. We’ll also check the new Hit Bound 7", 'Ghetto Rock' - another sharp collaboration linked to the A-Lone Ark camp, packed with live instrumentation and heavyweight musicianship.

And for the sound system heads, Roll & Record link with Small Axe on 'Warn Dem Again' - a tough modern cut steeped in 80s digital reggae DNA. Raw bass, stripped-back force, and built to shake boxes properly.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Studio One vaults opened up
item.description:
Five brand new Studio One 45s for 2026 - Larry Marshall, Hugh Godfrey, Lloyd Evans, Alton Ellis and Devon Russell, all backed with heavyweight versions. A key pairing sees Lloyd Evans’ I’m Losing You backed with Albert Tomlinson’s Don’t Wait For Me - foundation vocals, proper business.

Plus the killer new Digikiller reissue of The Heptones’ Get Up and Chant - a certified roots anthem, finally lifted off album duty and cut loud on a proper 12". On the flip, a serious bonus - a previously unreleased early 80s Stranger Cole tune in extended discomix style, stretched out for the system. Part of the Park Heights series - dug from the vaults and pressed right.

And finally dubstep from Aotearoa by Akcept ahead of his headline show at QQQ St in Moelbourne this Saturday. Foundation dubstep rooted in the early 2000s UK sound.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Ripe Fruits from the Lone Ark & The 18th Parallel
item.description:
This week on Riddim Yard: brand new roots pressure from Lone Ark meets The 18th Parallel with the heavyweight new 7” ‘Man Kill Man’ on Fruits Records. Recorded between Switzerland and Spain on 2” tape, Roberto Sánchez and crew dive deep into mid-70s roots reggae with live instrumentation, spiritual vocals and a serious anti-war message. Plus the dubwise flip ‘Peace Version’ mixed at A-Lone Ark Muzik Studio.

Also from the Fruits Records camp, Japanese saxophonist Yuko Arakawa makes her solo debut with the stunning instrumental ‘Kanata’. Inspired by Count Ossie, Cedric ‘Im’ Brooks and Nyahbinghi jazz reggae traditions, it’s a deep meditative piece carried by live horns, heavyweight rhythms and a swirling Mutron Bi-Phase dub mix from Lone Ark.

And representing the new school of Jamaican dancehall, fresh fire from Spanish Town deejay Skeng with the track ‘Dancehall Mad Man’. One of the leading voices in modern dancehall and one of the top current artists on Riddim Yard, Skeng continues to push raw street energy with a sound built for today’s dance.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: Roberto Sánchez reloads with more Lone Ark exclusives
item.description:
More exclusive pre-release vinyl straight from Roberto Sánchez's Lone Ark Studio in Spain. A fixture at the top of Riddim Yard's producer rankings for many years, Roberto returns with a heavyweight new 45 from Milton Henry - the Black Ark and Wackies veteran - titled Rastafari Can Not Die. Plus fresh 12" and 7" releases from Spanish roots outfit Irieginal Roots.

Also in the mix, new dancehall heat from rising artist Skari with Dancehall Style, alongside the latest from Jahvillani, a fresh gun tune in a modern dancehall style.

And from France, Stand High Patrol link up with UK singer Joe Yorke, bringing his trademark truesetto style and bass-heavy sound system vibrations.
item.author: Rick Howe

item.title: RIP Stranger Cole
item.description:
Paying tribute to our good friend Stranger Cole. One of the pioneering voices of the ska and rocksteady eras, who continued into the reggae era with a deep catalogue of quality music. Riddim Yard was lucky enough to host Stranger Cole twice on the program over the years. Wilburn Theodore Cole lived his life to fullest, bringing joy to everyone he met. RIP to a true musical legend.

Also on the program, brand new up to the times dancehall from wurl boss Vybz Kartel from his 19th studio album God & Time produced by TJ Records.
item.author: Rick Howe
